summary: PR 2337 made permuter-driven matching fixes in Yoshi special-move code and substantial Link arrow item work. The bot report records +5220 matched-code bytes, +256 matched-data bytes, 9 new matches, full .data match for itlinkarrow, and several newly matched or improved functions. Human review comments were not present in the loaded slice.

key_files.role: Primary item decomp/matching work: added includes, adjusted fake JObj helper assert filename handling, converted casts and locals, introduced address-named float arrays, rewrote fn_802A81C4 and it_802A850C shapes, and contributed most reported matches.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: itlinkarrow.c +253/-311 in 25 hunks
diff.diff: itlinkarrow.c includes placeholder.h, baselib/forward.h, baselib/gobj.h
diff.diff: added __FILE__ override to jobj.h around fake_HSD_JObjSetRotation/fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate and restored itlinkarrow.c
diff.diff: numbers/numbers_l removed; it_803F6A78 and it_803F6A98 added
diff.diff: fn_802A81C4 changed from void HSD_GObj* to s32 Item_GObj* and uses GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists itlinkarrow .data 32.13% to 100.00%, several itlinkarrow function matches, and improvements

key_files.role: Header signature and extern surface updated for Link arrow work.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: itlinkarrow.h +2/-1 in 2 hunks
diff.diff: fn_802A81C4 prototype changed to s32 fn_802A81C4(Item_GObj*)
diff.diff: added extern f32 it_803F6A78[]

key_files.role: Yoshi SpecialS matching fixes using small permuter helper inlines, expression reshaping, stack padding changes, and ABS/assignment simplification.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftYs_SpecialS.c +47/-34 in 16 hunks
diff.diff: added perm_vel_inline, perm_neg_inline, perm_mul_inline, perm_vel_x_inline with comment 'permuter-generated helpers, almost certainly fake'
diff.diff: changed f64 casts in angle/rotation expressions to simpler M_PI/M_PI_2 expressions
diff.diff: PAD_STACK(4) changed to u8 pad[4]; PAD_STACK(40) changed to u8 pad[28]
diff.diff: fp->mv.ys.specials.x1C = ABS(fp->gr_vel = fp->self_vel.x)
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists Yoshi SpecialS new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Small local variable lifetime/name reshaping in ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftYs_SpecialHi.c +5/-5 in 1 hunk
diff.diff: temp_f4 renamed/reworked as mag and reused across the angle calculation
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C +1 byte improvement, 97.06% to 97.22%

what_changed.text: Link arrow item code was heavily reshaped, including function signatures, casts, helper usage, local names, stack padding, and data arrays.

what_changed.text: Yoshi SpecialS received narrow permuter-oriented expression and local-shape changes that the bot report associates with three newly matched collision functions and one improved animation function.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftYs_SpecialS.c perm_* inline helpers and expression changes
text_corpus.jsonl: ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_Coll, _1_Coll, _3_Coll reached 100%; ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_Anim improved to 95.86%

what_changed.text: Yoshi SpecialHi ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C changed a temporary float into a reused mag variable and slightly improved according to the bot report.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftYs_SpecialHi.c temp_f4 to mag
text_corpus.jsonl: ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C +1 byte improvement

what_changed.text: Automated report recorded 9 new matches, including itlinkarrow .data reaching 100%, several Link arrow functions, and three Yoshi SpecialS collision functions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev[bot] report for GALE01

smart_moves.text: The Link arrow header was updated when fn_802A81C4's recovered body/signature changed, rather than hiding the signature locally.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: itlinkarrow.h changes fn_802A81C4 to s32 fn_802A81C4(Item_GObj*)
diff.diff: itlinkarrow.c defines s32 fn_802A81C4(Item_GObj* gobj)

smart_moves.text: Several unnecessary HSD_GObj casts were removed where Item_GObj/HSD_GObj-compatible call sites already accepted the expression shape used in the PR.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: GET_ITEM(gobj), it_80275158(gobj,...), Item_8026AB54(gobj,...), return gobj in it_802A83E0
diff.diff: itLinkArrow_Logic98_EvtUnk changes it_8026B894(gobj, (HSD_GObj*) arg1) to it_8026B894(gobj, arg1)

smart_moves.text: A repeated copy block in it_802A7E40 stayed as a compact loop rather than expanded assignments.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: removed #if/#else assignment block and kept for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) pointer-index copy

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered function body proves a narrower or more accurate signature, update the owning header in the same PR.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: fn_802A81C4 definition changed to s32 fn_802A81C4(Item_GObj*)
diff.diff: itlinkarrow.h prototype updated accordingly

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small artificial inline wrappers can be used as matching tactics, but the PR evidence labels some of them as permuter-generated and 'almost certainly fake', so they should be treated as narrow tactics rather than source style.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: comment before perm_neg_inline/perm_mul_inline/perm_vel_x_inline says 'permuter-generated helpers, almost certainly fake'
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report shows Yoshi collision functions reached 100%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Assert filename evidence can force source-shape compromises: this PR temporarily overrides __FILE__ around fake JObj helpers to emit jobj.h assert locations, then restores itlinkarrow.c.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: #undef __FILE__ / #define __FILE__ "jobj.h" before fake_HSD_JObjSetRotation
diff.diff: #undef __FILE__ / #define __FILE__ "itlinkarrow.c" after fake helpers

naming_conventions.text: Local object pointers were renamed to short role names such as ip for Item* and jobj for HSD_JObj* in itlinkarrow work.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: fn_802A81C4 uses Item* ip and HSD_JObj* jobj
diff.diff: it_802A850C uses Item* ip and HSD_JObj* jobj

naming_conventions.text: The SpecialHi temporary name was changed from register-style temp_f4 to role-ish mag once its value represented input magnitude.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftYs_SpecialHi.c float temp_f4 changed to float mag

naming_conventions.text: Recovered data arrays in itlinkarrow were address-style named it_803F6A78 and it_803F6A98 rather than semantic names.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: added f32 it_803F6A78[] and f32 it_803F6A98[]
diff.diff: itlinkarrow.h added extern f32 it_803F6A78[]

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Introduced tiny inline access/math wrappers such as perm_vel_inline, perm_neg_inline, perm_mul_inline, and perm_vel_x_inline to steer codegen for Yoshi SpecialS.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftYs_SpecialS.c adds perm_vel_inline returning fp->gr_vel
diff.diff: ftYs_SpecialS.c adds perm_neg_inline, perm_mul_inline, perm_vel_x_inline
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists Yoshi SpecialS functions newly matched/improved

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Adjusted explicit stack padding forms and sizes in Yoshi and Link arrow functions.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftYs_SpecialAirSLanding_Anim PAD_STACK(4) to u8 pad[4]
diff.diff: ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Coll PAD_STACK(40) to u8 pad[28]
diff.diff: it_802A850C introduces several u8 _pad arrays and PAD_STACK(12)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used M_PI_2 and simpler float expressions instead of explicit f64 casts in several angle calculations.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_Anim removes f64 casts around M_PI and M_PI_2 expressions
diff.diff: it_802A850C uses M_PI_2 * ip->facing_dir

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used a compact assignment inside ABS for gr_vel/self_vel synchronization in ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Coll.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: fp->mv.ys.specials.x1C = ABS(fp->gr_vel = fp->self_vel.x)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Link arrow data matching involved removing generic numbers/numbers_l arrays and adding address-style float arrays near the state table.

diff.diff: added f32 it_803F6A78[] and f32 it_803F6A98[]

notes: The diff evidence is truncated for the large itlinkarrow.c file, so some detailed changed hunks are not represented in this postmortem.
There is no PR body explanation and no human review feedback in the loaded slice.
The bot report provides validation-like matching evidence, but no local build logs, objdiff command output, or reviewer rationale are included.
The PR itself contains comments calling some permuter helpers fake; this supports classifying them as tactics, not accepted decomp style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header signatures should track recovered bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2337 changed fn_802A81C4 to s32 fn_802A81C4(Item_GObj*) in both itlinkarrow.c and itlinkarrow.h, supporting the reusable lesson that recovered signatures should be updated in the owning header.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: itlinkarrow.c fn_802A81C4 definition
diff.diff: itlinkarrow.h prototype update

accepted_candidate_records.title: Permuter helper inlines are narrow tactics, not style
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2337 used permuter helper inlines in ftYs_SpecialS.c and explicitly commented that some were 'permuter-generated helpers, almost certainly fake'; treat similar helpers as matching tactics needing cleanup review.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: perm_* helper definitions and comment
text_corpus.jsonl: Yoshi SpecialS functions newly matched/improved

source_update_candidates.title: Document fake permuter helper wrappers as cleanup-sensitive tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2337 provides historical evidence of simple helper wrappers named perm_* being used for matching while also labeled 'almost certainly fake'. Consider adding or cross-linking guidance that such wrappers must remain narrow, evidence-backed tactics and should not be promoted to normal authored style.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftYs_SpecialS.c comment 'permuter-generated helpers, almost certainly fake'
diff.diff: perm_neg_inline, perm_mul_inline, perm_vel_x_inline definitions
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report Yoshi SpecialS matches
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with existing standards but may be useful as a searchable, explicit example for curator review.

source_update_candidates.title: Assert filename overrides around fake header helpers need strict scoping
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2337 temporarily redefined __FILE__ to jobj.h around fake HSD_JObj helpers and restored itlinkarrow.c afterward. Consider documenting this as a risky matching workaround that should be scoped tightly and revisited when real header inline/helper recovery becomes possible.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: __FILE__ set to "jobj.h" around fake_HSD_JObjSetRotation/fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate
diff.diff: __FILE__ restored to "itlinkarrow.c"
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already prefer header inlines/macros; this PR is a concrete historical example of a temporary workaround.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that perm_* inline helpers are accepted style; PR evidence calls some fake and the loaded standards require explicit matching evidence for such tactics.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale because no human review comments were loaded.
Do not infer all itlinkarrow.c changed functions from the truncated diff; only the visible hunks and bot report are summarized.
Do not promote it_803F6A78 or it_803F6A98 semantic meaning beyond address-style float arrays; no semantic naming evidence was loaded.
